data.title: Lifeward launches sales of ReWalk 7 personal exoskeleton in U.S.
data.description:
• Lifeward (NASDAQ:LFWD) on Tuesday announced the U.S. national launch of the ReWalk 7 Personal Exoskeleton, the next generation of its personal exoskeleton technology. 
  • The company will now begin sales of ReWalk 7, which includes new and enhanced features such as cloud connectivity and customizable walking speeds. 
  • As part of the FDA clearance process, Lifeward worked with more than two dozen end users and physical therapists, respectively, to gather critical feedback on the impact of the device in both the rehabilitative and real-world settings.
  • The ReWalk 7 is now available for purchase in the U.S. for personal use, as well as in the clinical setting.
  • Lifeward (NASDAQ:LFWD) shares were up 2.78% in premarket trading.

data.title: Empire State Manufacturing gauge climbs more than expected in April, but still in negative terrain
data.description: The Empire State Manufacturing Index rose to -8.10 from -20.00 in March, compared with the -12.80 consensus, according to a New York Federal Reserve survey released on Tuesday.

data.title: PepsiCo dips after Bank of America heads to the sidelines despite the consumer staples revival
data.description:
Bank of America lowered its rating on PepsiCo (NASDAQ:PEP) to Neutral from Buy on Tuesday.

While the market rotation by investors into consumer staples is seen as providing some support for PepsiCo, BofA sees limited scope for a meaningful valuation re-rating on the stock until the market regains confidence that sales and profit growth will match higher-valued large-cap staples peers.

Analyst Bryan Spillane warned specifically that PepsiCo's (NASDAQ:PEP) Frito-Lay North America growth is likely to remain below long-term trends this year. "While international segments are scaling and making large contributions to sales and profit growth, it’s not enough at this point to make up forslower growth at FLNA," he highlighted. Looking ahead, Spillane and his team see limited opportunity for either topline outperformance for PepsiCo (PEP) or for better than low single-digit EPS growth in 2025 and 2026.

BofA lowered its FY26 EPS estimate on PepsiCo (PEP) to $8.70 from $8.85 and now sees FY27 EPS of $9.27 from a prior view for $9.45. The firm assigned a price objective of $155.

Shares of PepsiCo (PEP) were down 1.2% in premarket trading to $145.00 vs. the 52-week range of $138.33 to $183.41. The dividend yield for new buyers of the stock is 3.69%.

data.title: HubSpot in focus as UBS upgrades on back of 'attractive' set-up
data.description:
HubSpot (NYSE:HUBS) was in focus on Tuesday as UBS upgraded the marketing software firm, citing an “attractive” set-up.

Shares rose 1.6% in premarket trading.

“HUBS shares are down 35% since mid-Feb and with the valuation now at 9x CY25E EV/S (below 2-yr avg. of 11x), we're upgrading our outlook from Neutral to Buy given our view that the set-up is attractive for a high-quality software company who has a more conservative FY25 guide (less likely to need a material cut) and could be a snapback long if macro headwinds prove to be overblown,” analyst Taylor McGinnis wrote in a note to clients. “We believe HUBS' company-specific growth tailwinds are underappreciated and could help to curb downside pressures in the near-term and support durable growth in the long-term.”

These tailwinds include a move upmarket, multi-Hub adoption from vendor consolidation and a change to its pricing model, McGinnis said.

And while there have been some concerns about tariffs, McGinnis said HubSpot is unlikely to be impacted by them, at least initially.

“Post tariff news, the partners haven't seen a significant impact to spend/pipeline yet and it seemed too early for FY25 growth revisions, but a couple noted more hesitation/deal cycles lengthening and the tone down-ticked modestly,” McGinnis added. “1QF25 seemed largely in-line, but we’re prudently assuming some deal delays/disruption ahead as some [small and medium-sized] enter wait/see mode.”
